economic_notes: Consolidated entities include We'koqma'q One Stop, We'koqma'q Housing Projects, and We'koqma'q Contracting Incorporated. Trout farm inventory recorded at market value. First Nations Fisheries Interest Group Partnership promissory note receivable at 6.14%. Snow crab sales with Premium Seafood Group.
notes: Three consolidated subsidiaries: We'koqma'q One Stop, We'koqma'q Housing Projects, We'koqma'q Contracting Incorporated. ISC funding of $2,627,650 is only the receivable from ISC shown in Note 2; total government transfers per income statement is $23,695,111. CMHC replacement reserve deficiency of $804,208. Capital lease obligations of $51,975. Promissory note receivable of $17,580,361 from First Nations Fisheries Interest Group Partnership. Bad debt expense of $500,846 recorded in 2022. Inventory decreased significantly from $6,487,899 to $2,240,969 mainly due to trout farm reduction.
